title: Cosmos Oscilloscope — mic-reactive glowing time-domain waveform
summary: An oscilloscope visualizer that reacts to the MICROPHONE live: multiple stacked, harmonics-tinted time-domain waveform traces with a soft trail and a center glow that pulses on beats. Gracefully falls back to a synthetic signal if the mic is unavailable or denied, plus a deterministic synthetic demo for capture. Self-contained, zero dependencies.
usage_notes: RUN: open and click "enable mic" to drive the scope with your voice or music; if no mic is granted it falls back to a built-in beat. KEY TECHNIQUES: live AnalyserNode getByteTimeDomainData / getByteFrequencyData feeds render(spectrum, level, t); a deterministic synthSpectrum(u) (seeded sum-of-sines formants + beat kick) feeds the SAME render() for auto and fallback; stacked harmonic traces shaped by the spectrum, additive glow, trailing fade. CAPTURE: ?auto=1 is synthetic + window.__seek(t). TUNE: TRACES, COL tints, ampBase, LOOP, the formant centers f1/f2/f3 and beat count in synthSpectrum.
